title: The Sting

jokes.text: The only sound is Bender calling everyone else a jerk as he swings! And he slices it... into the water, duh! Man!
jokes.setup: Golf announcer building tension about Bender's swing
jokes.punchline: Bender calling everyone jerks while playing terribly
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender behavior - antisocial while failing at something. Good character integration.

jokes.text: Bad news, everyone! You're not good enough to go on your next mission.
jokes.setup: Professor's typical 'good news everyone' opening
jokes.punchline: Subverted to 'bad news' with blunt insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion of Professor's catchphrase with character-appropriate bluntness.

jokes.text: We live to suck another day!
jokes.setup: Being told they're not good enough for dangerous mission
jokes.punchline: Twisting 'live to fight another day' to acknowledge their incompetence
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that perfectly captures the crew's self-aware incompetence.

jokes.text: That doesn't sound so dangerous. This is no ordinary honey! It's produced by vicious space bees.
jokes.setup: Mission described as collecting 'ordinary honey'
jokes.punchline: Immediate revelation it's from deadly space bees
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection setup but fairly predictable for sci-fi comedy.

jokes.text: And that's if you're not allergic! You don't wanna know what happens then oh no no, God no! Your insides will boil out of your eye sockets like a science fair volcano!
jokes.setup: Professor warning about bee stings causing instant death
jokes.punchline: Graphic, absurd description of allergic reaction
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with vivid, absurd imagery. The 'science fair volcano' comparison is inspired.

jokes.text: I didn't want to know!
jokes.setup: Professor's graphic description after saying 'you don't wanna know'
jokes.punchline: Crew member's delayed protest
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction to Professor's over-sharing.

jokes.text: These bees are larger than most Buicks! and twice as ugly. Larger than an American sedan?
jokes.setup: Professor describing bee size using car comparison
jokes.punchline: Crew member's confused clarification about car types
jokes.explanation: Mild confusion humor but not particularly sharp or memorable.

jokes.text: How big is the honeycomb? Honeycomb's big. Yeah, yeah, yeah! It's not small? No, no, no.
jokes.setup: Question about honeycomb size
jokes.punchline: Professor's sing-song cereal commercial parody response
jokes.explanation: Clever reference to Honeycomb cereal jingle in absurd context. Pop culture integration works well.

jokes.text: But Leela, we're no good! Listen, I'm scared too. But I'm more scared of disappointing myself.
jokes.setup: Crew protesting they're incompetent
jokes.punchline: Leela's misplaced priorities - self-disappointment vs death
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leela characterization - her pride and determination overriding common sense.

jokes.text: I'm not scared of that at all!
jokes.setup: Leela saying she's scared of disappointing herself
jokes.punchline: Fry's honest admission he has no fear of self-disappointment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry moment - his low expectations and self-awareness delivered matter-of-factly.

jokes.text: Now, bees communicate by dancing. Like my parents! Wait! That was hitting!
jokes.setup: Professor explaining bee communication through dancing
jokes.punchline: Amy's innocent comparison followed by dark correction
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with dark family dysfunction revealed through innocent comparison.

jokes.text: I often have thoughts and feelings that can only be expressed through dance!
jokes.setup: Learning about bee language cartridge for dancing
jokes.punchline: Fry's sincere artistic pretensions about dance expression
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's unexpected depth and artistic side.

jokes.text: Bender! Quick! Waggle something!
jokes.setup: Bee attack requiring communication through dance
jokes.punchline: Desperate command for Bender to dance/waggle
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy setup with 'waggle' being an amusing verb choice.

jokes.text: Leela, let's turn back, there's absolutely no shame in wussing out.
jokes.setup: Approaching dangerous bee hive
jokes.punchline: Fry trying to reframe cowardice as reasonable
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry cowardice presented as wisdom. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Well I aim to prove him wrong. It'll take more than a few deadly deadly bees to... Couldn't make that out. Too much static!
jokes.setup: Dead crew captain's defiant final words
jokes.punchline: Recording cuts out at the moment of death/failure
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy timing with the recording conveniently cutting out at the worst moment.

jokes.text: Hey, what's this goop? Royal jelly. They feed it to the queen. Bees made honey and jelly? How come nothing humans make tastes good?
jokes.setup: Learning bees produce both honey and royal jelly
jokes.punchline: Fry's observation about human food inferiority
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor with self-deprecating human comparison. Relatable and well-timed.

jokes.text: Help! I can't swim in jelly as far as I know!
jokes.setup: Fry falling into royal jelly
jokes.punchline: His uncertain qualification about his jelly-swimming abilities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry logic - being uncertain about impossible abilities. The qualifier 'as far as I know' is brilliant.

jokes.text: You adorable wittle itty bitty fuzzy wuzzy...
jokes.setup: Leela discovering cute baby queen bee
jokes.punchline: Tough Leela using excessive baby talk
jokes.explanation: Standard character contradiction humor - tough character going soft. Well-executed but predictable.

jokes.text: Leela, stop bonding and let's go! I got jelly in my underpants!
jokes.setup: Fry fell in royal jelly while Leela is distracted by baby bee
jokes.punchline: His uncomfortable physical predicament
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy moment but relies on basic bodily discomfort humor.

jokes.text: I didn't mean you're all fat. It's just fatso there.
jokes.setup: Fry apparently making insensitive comments to angry bees
jokes.punchline: His attempt to clarify only makes it worse by singling out one bee
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry foot-in-mouth moment, making bad situation worse with poor word choice.

jokes.text: We made it, we're alive! Burn out that old crew! The only things they did better than us were suck and die.
jokes.setup: Successfully escaping the bee hive
jokes.punchline: Mocking the dead crew with backhanded compliment
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with good structure - the punchline reframes their 'superiority' as just not being dead yet.

jokes.text: I don't want anything to happen to you. Thanks, but I can look out for myself. Frankly, I can smell danger a mile away. Look out!
jokes.setup: Leela boasting about her danger-sensing abilities
jokes.punchline: Immediate danger appearing right as she makes the claim
jokes.explanation: Classic timing joke - character's boast immediately undermined by reality. Well-structured but predictable.

jokes.text: You call that a wound? That's a boo boo tops! But thanks for trying to save me, Fry!
jokes.setup: Fry apparently getting fatally stung protecting Leela
jokes.punchline: Leela dismissing her minor injury while acknowledging Fry's sacrifice
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leela's toughness contrasted with gratitude for Fry's heroism.

jokes.text: Who will make Bender waffles just the way he likes them now?
jokes.setup: Fry apparently dying from bee sting
jokes.punchline: Bender's selfish concern about his breakfast
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender characterization - making Fry's death about his own convenience. Excellent character integration.

jokes.text: I'm lying to make her feel better!
jokes.setup: Professor presumably comforting Leela about Fry's death
jokes.punchline: Immediately admitting his comfort was false
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-humor with Professor breaking the fourth wall of comfort. Perfect character trait of brutal honesty.

jokes.text: Husband, can't you go anywhere without lighting something up? It's an old Jamaican accounting tradition. We burn his timecard. That way, his zombie doesn't come back looking for his final paycheck.
jokes.setup: LaBarbara questioning Hermes lighting something at funeral
jokes.punchline: Absurd bureaucratic zombie prevention ritual
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor combining Hermes' bureaucracy obsession with zombie mythology. Highly original and character-specific.

jokes.text: He looks so natural. It's funny to think he's crammed full of sawdust and preservatives.
jokes.setup: Amy commenting on how natural Fry looks at funeral
jokes.punchline: Revealing the grotesque embalming details
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor juxtaposing appearance with reality. The 'it's funny to think' framing works well.

jokes.text: I barely knew Philip, but as a clergyman I have no problems telling his most intimate friends all about him.
jokes.setup: Funeral officiant beginning eulogy
jokes.punchline: Admitting ignorance while claiming authority to speak about deceased
jokes.explanation: Excellent satire of generic funeral services and clerical authority. Sharp observation about impersonal ceremonies.

jokes.text: Soothe us with sweet lies.
jokes.setup: Reverend offering to comfort them about Fry
jokes.punchline: Leela's blunt request for comforting falsehoods
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on funeral comfort. Perfect economy and self-awareness about the ritual lies we tell.

jokes.text: It may comfort you to know Fry's death took only 15 seconds. Yet the pain was so intense that it felt to him like 15 years. And it goes without saying, it caused him to empty his bowels.
jokes.setup: Reverend trying to provide comfort about Fry's death
jokes.punchline: Each 'comforting' detail making it progressively worse
jokes.explanation: Masterful escalation of horrific details masquerading as comfort. Each revelation is worse than the last.

jokes.text: This makes me the oldest member of my family.
jokes.setup: Fry's death at his own funeral
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact observation about family hierarchy
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about death with Fry's characteristic matter-of-fact delivery. Good character voice.

jokes.text: Him do good snoo-snoo.
jokes.setup: Amazon warrior at Fry's funeral
jokes.punchline: Eulogy reduced to sex reference in broken English
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier episode works for fans, though relies heavily on previous context.

jokes.text: All those times, I said 'Kill all humans', I'd always whisper 'except one'. Fry was that one... and I never told him so.
jokes.setup: Bender's typical anti-human rhetoric
jokes.punchline: Secret exception for Fry reveals hidden affection
jokes.explanation: Exceptional character moment revealing Bender's hidden heart. Perfect subversion of his catchphrase with genuine emotion.

jokes.text: I thought you were dead. No, I'm better than ever. Before the accident, I couldn't do this! Impressive. But it does sort of support my You-Are-Dead theory.
jokes.setup: Fry appearing to Leela after his death, demonstrating new abilities
jokes.punchline: Professor's logical conclusion that supernatural abilities prove death
jokes.explanation: Good logical absurdity with Professor's scientific approach to supernatural phenomena.

jokes.text: There is a surprise for you in my locker. I got it at Swedish novelty shop before he left.
jokes.setup: Fry claiming to prove he's alive with secret knowledge
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific Swedish novelty shop detail
jokes.explanation: The Swedish novelty shop is an amusing random detail, though not particularly strong comedy.

jokes.text: Of course he still exists. As a frozen corpse in outer space! I made myself sad.
jokes.setup: Leela believing Fry still exists in some form
jokes.punchline: Professor's literal interpretation followed by self-awareness
jokes.explanation: Excellent Professor moment - scientific literalism followed by unexpected emotional awareness.

jokes.text: Bupkis! Well, I feel better now. Pawning all this stuff in Fry's locker helped give me closure.
jokes.setup: Finding nothing special in Fry's locker
jokes.punchline: Bender's mercenary attitude toward grief and closure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - turning grief into profit opportunity. The 'closure' psychology twisted to justify theft.

jokes.text: Was there anything in there that might have been a gift for me? No. Nothing. Not at all. No way jose! Here it is. You took this from Fry's locker!? Hey, the guy's dead. There's no law against grave robbing!
jokes.setup: Leela asking about potential gift, Bender's emphatic denials
jokes.punchline: Immediate revelation and Bender's legal justification
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with Bender's over-the-top denials immediately undermined. His casual attitude toward grave robbing is perfect.

jokes.text: If Fry is out there somewhere, Leela's brain could be acting like a five-pound ouija board.
jokes.setup: Explaining possible psychic connection to dead Fry
jokes.punchline: Reducing brain to spiritual communication device with specific weight
jokes.explanation: Clever scientific absurdity with the specific weight detail. Good Professor pseudo-science.

jokes.text: Is this some sort of brain scanner? Some sort, yes. In France it's called a guillotine.
jokes.setup: Leela asking about medical equipment
jokes.punchline: Professor's casual admission it's execution device
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark misdirection with perfect Professor nonchalance about using deadly equipment.

jokes.text: Hey! What if Fry wasn't actually dead when we buried him? You know, like Julia Roberts.
jokes.setup: Amy suggesting Fry might have been buried alive
jokes.punchline: Comparing to Julia Roberts movie instead of actual premature burial cases
jokes.explanation: Weak pop culture reference that doesn't add much. The Julia Roberts comparison is arbitrary.

jokes.text: How could I have known the gift was in Fry's locker? Easy. He told you about it before he died and your grief-roasted mind blocked it out!
jokes.setup: Leela questioning her supernatural knowledge
jokes.punchline: Professor's 'grief-roasted' psychological explanation
jokes.explanation: 'Grief-roasted' is an excellent invented term that perfectly captures the Professor's pseudo-scientific style.

jokes.text: Are you saying...I'm going crazy? No! No one's saying that. But I'm certainly thinking it loudly.
jokes.setup: Leela's concern about her mental state
jokes.punchline: Professor's distinction between saying and thinking
jokes.explanation: Excellent Professor logic - technically truthful while being brutally honest. Perfect character consistency.

jokes.text: Could a dream take you dancing in an illusion garden? Well, yes technically. But it's still very sweet of you.
jokes.setup: Fry trying to prove he's real through romantic gestures
jokes.punchline: Leela's logical admission followed by appreciation anyway
jokes.explanation: Good logical deflation of romantic gesture while maintaining the emotional core.

jokes.text: It's all so beautiful. I've forgot how many hundreds of degrees below zero it must be.
jokes.setup: Romantic ice planet setting
jokes.punchline: Leela's practical observation about deadly temperature
jokes.explanation: Typical Leela practicality undermining romantic moment. Character-consistent but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Quit your bragging, my boyfriend's alive too. Not anymore.
jokes.setup: Amy responding to Leela's claim about Fry being alive
jokes.punchline: LaBarbara's casual revelation about killing her boyfriend
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with perfect economy. The casual delivery makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: I'm from Jamaica the show-me island, so show me you're blowing it out your fanny.
jokes.setup: Hermes demanding proof of Leela's claims
jokes.punchline: Mixing 'show-me state' with Jamaica and crude dismissal
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay mixing geography with skepticism, though somewhat forced.

jokes.text: I am not under stress, damn it!
jokes.setup: Being told she's under stress
jokes.punchline: Angrily denying stress while clearly stressed
jokes.explanation: Classic contradiction humor but very predictable. The execution is competent but uninspired.

jokes.text: A little? You're screwier than my aunt Rita. She's a screw.
jokes.setup: Responding to Leela admitting she's freaking out a little
jokes.punchline: Family comparison with literal mechanical interpretation
jokes.explanation: Excellent Bender wordplay combining family references with his robot nature. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: Be careful you purple haired imbecile! One spoonful calms you down. Two spoonfuls help you sleep. But three spoonfuls and you're going into a sleep so deep... you're never wake up. Never!
jokes.setup: Warning about space honey dosage
jokes.punchline: Nursery rhyme structure with lethal consequences
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with children's rhyme structure building to death. The rhythm and structure work perfectly.

jokes.text: Oh my gosh! I'd better get a sponge!
jokes.setup: Leela taking the third lethal spoonful of honey
jokes.punchline: Domestic concern about cleaning up mess before dying
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leela practicality - worrying about cleaning even while overdosing. Good character moment.

jokes.text: Why am I sticky and naked? Did I miss something fun?
jokes.setup: Fry waking up in royal jelly confused about his condition
jokes.punchline: His assumption that naked and sticky means he had fun
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry innocence and crude humor. His optimistic interpretation is very character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Neat! This is great! My buddy is alive and his credit cards are valuable again!
jokes.setup: Fry being resurrected from royal jelly
jokes.punchline: Bender's excitement about restored financial opportunities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - friendship is great but the real value is financial exploitation. Excellent character consistency.

jokes.text: In every life we have some trouble. But when you worry, you make it double. Don't worry, be happy! The landlord says your rent is late. You may have to litigate. But don't worry! Bee happy!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg trying to cheer up Leela
jokes.punchline: Bobby McFerrin song parody with 'bee' pun and legal troubles
jokes.explanation: Decent song parody with bee theme connection. The legal troubles addition is amusing but slightly forced.

jokes.text: Were you just singing? No. I was telling you not to worry. I'm not allowed to sing. Court order.
jokes.setup: Amy questioning Zoidberg's musical performance
jokes.punchline: Legal prohibition on Zoidberg's singing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor. The idea that Zoidberg's singing is so bad it's legally prohibited is inspired character comedy.

jokes.text: I'll find Fry's coffin, get his corpse and keep it under my mattress to remind me that he's really dead. That'll prove I'm not insane!
jokes.setup: Leela trying to prove her sanity
jokes.punchline: Proposing extremely insane behavior to demonstrate sanity
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic logic - the plan to prove sanity is completely insane. Perfect psychological breakdown humor.

jokes.text: OK, I'm insane. But I'm still sane enough to know it. The only time I feel alright is in my dreams. With you. 3 spoonfuls, then...I can dream forever.
jokes.setup: Leela accepting her mental state
jokes.punchline: Choosing permanent escape through honey-induced coma
jokes.explanation: Dark character moment with good callback to earlier warning. The paradox of being sane enough to know you're insane works well.

jokes.text: I've tried it. Bedsores hurt!
jokes.setup: Warning Leela against lying in bed like a vegetable
jokes.punchline: Bender admits personal experience with extreme laziness
jokes.explanation: Good Bender moment combining concern with personal experience

jokes.text: Listen to me! You don't wanna lie in bed like a vegetable and do nothing the rest of your life. I've tried it. Bedsores hurt!
jokes.setup: Bender trying to talk Leela out of permanent coma
jokes.punchline: His personal experience with extreme laziness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender logic - opposing the coma based on personal experience with laziness rather than concern for Leela.

jokes.text: My new spleen came from a guy who liked motorcycles.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he survived the bee sting
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to organ donor's dangerous lifestyle
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about organ donation with matter-of-fact delivery

jokes.text: Sweet three-toed sloth of ice planet Hoth! She's awake!
jokes.setup: Professor reacting to Leela waking up
jokes.punchline: Elaborate sci-fi exclamation mixing Star Wars reference
jokes.explanation: Creative sci-fi exclamation with Star Wars reference. The elaborate construction is amusing though slightly forced.

jokes.text: And he talked non-stop, like a parrot of the sea he was.
jokes.setup: Describing Fry's constant talking during Leela's coma
jokes.punchline: Mixing parrot with nautical metaphor
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay mixing bird and sea metaphors. Decent but not particularly strong or memorable.

jokes.text: You could really use a shower. You too.
jokes.setup: Emotional reunion after Leela's recovery
jokes.punchline: Immediate return to practical hygiene concerns
jokes.explanation: Simple callback to earlier hygiene issues. Ends episode on practical note after emotional climax.

tmdb_overview: The Planet Express crew attempt the mission that killed the last crew: raiding a massive alien beehive for its honey.
